name: 蔡淑君

person_relationships.description: Appointed as alternate member of Council of Presidential Advisers by President Halimah Yacob in 2019

political_stances.stance_content: Called for regulators to grant telecom companies the right to charge technology companies such as WhatsApp and Skype for use of telecom networks, arguing it was necessary due to falling revenues from phone calls and text messaging

major_achievements.achievement: Instrumental in the corporatisation of Singtel
Led Singtel's successful listing on the Singapore Stock Exchange
Restructured the company for competition ahead of full liberalisation of Singapore telecoms market
Led Singtel's internationalisation strategy including acquisition of Optus (Australia) and investments in Bharti Airtel (India), Telkomsel (Indonesia), AIS (Thailand), and Globe (Philippines)
Transformed Singtel from a traditional telecommunications company into a communications technology company, connecting 468-700 million mobile customers across 21 countries
Conferred the Public Service Star (BBM) at Singapore's National Day Awards
Conferred the Medal of Commendation (Gold) at NTUC May Day Awards

biography_summary: Chua Sock Koong BBM (born 14 September 1957) is a Singaporean business executive who served as Group Chief Executive Officer of Singapore Telecommunications Limited (Singtel) from April 2007 to December 2020. She joined Singtel in 1989 as Treasurer, became CFO in 1999, and rose to Group CEO in 2007. She was instrumental in Singtel's corporatisation, its stock exchange listing, and its internationalisation strategy including the Optus acquisition and regional telecom investments across Asia. She is a Fellow of the Institute of Singapore Chartered Accountants and holds a CFA charter. She currently serves as a member of the Council of Presidential Advisers and Deputy Chairman of the Public Service Commission.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with detailed career timeline, board memberships, awards, and political stance on telecom regulation. Birth date corrected from 1956-01-01 to 1957-09-14. Education expanded with degree details. No social media accounts found.
